title: viola suavis subsp. adriatica
intro: Viola suavis subsp. adriatica is a subspecies of the sweet violet (Viola suavis) plant, a perennial herb native to the Adriatic region in Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Viola suavis subsp. adriatica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote better sleep. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds.
